explicit_text: My family's roots trace back to Andalusia, specifically a small village in Granada called Cuevas del Campo. Andalusia was where my ancestors lived before my father emigrated in the 1960s seeking work in Barcelona. It holds a special place in my heart as the origin of our family history and culture.

explicit_text: Hospitalet de Llobregat is where my brother Ricardo and I were born after my family moved from La Verneda. Hospitalet represents my childhood and the continuation of my family's life in Catalonia.

explicit_text: Paquillo was the bus company that transported emigrants like my family from Barcelona to our village in Cuevas del Campo every summer. The trip took between 12 and 15 hours, stopping in many villages along the way. This service was essential for maintaining our connection to our roots and visiting family back home.

explicit_text: In 1991, I participated in an exchange program called Amity Aid for teachers in Minnesota, United States. This program was a turning point that led me to live in Saint Paul, Minnesota, where I have been since. Amity Aid opened new opportunities for me beyond Spain and connected me to a new life abroad.
